superclasses.name: Heteroatom Alkylation and Arylation
superclasses.description: Reactions involving the formation of bonds between heteroatoms and carbon atoms

classes.name: N-alkylation
classes.description: Formation of bonds between nitrogen atoms and alkyl groups

subclasses.name: Reductive amination
subclasses.description: N-alkylation via condensation of an amine with an aldehyde or ketone followed by reduction of the resulting imine or iminium ion

subclasses.name: SN2 N-alkylation
subclasses.description: Direct nucleophilic substitution of an alkyl halide or sulfonate by an amine

subclasses.name: Mitsunobu N-alkylation
subclasses.description: N-alkylation of amines, amides, or N-heterocycles using an alcohol activated by DIAD/PPh3

subclasses.name: Epoxide ring opening with amines
subclasses.description: Ring opening of an epoxide by an amine nucleophile to form a β-amino alcohol

classes.name: O-alkylation
classes.description: Formation of bonds between oxygen atoms and alkyl groups

subclasses.name: SN2 O-alkylation
subclasses.description: Nucleophilic substitution of an alkyl halide or sulfonate by an alkoxide or phenoxide

subclasses.name: Mitsunobu O-alkylation
subclasses.description: O-alkylation of alcohols or phenols with a second alcohol under Mitsunobu conditions (DIAD/PPh3)

subclasses.name: Epoxide ring opening with alcohols/phenols
subclasses.description: Ring opening of an epoxide by an oxygen nucleophile to form an ether or diol derivative

classes.name: S-alkylation
classes.description: Formation of bonds between sulfur atoms and alkyl groups

subclasses.name: SN2 S-alkylation
subclasses.description: Nucleophilic substitution of an alkyl halide or sulfonate by a thiolate

subclasses.name: Epoxide ring opening with thiols
subclasses.description: Ring opening of an epoxide by a thiol nucleophile to form a β-hydroxy thioether

classes.name: N-arylation
classes.description: Formation of bonds between nitrogen atoms and aryl groups (Buchwald-Hartwig)

subclasses.name: Buchwald-Hartwig amination
subclasses.description: Palladium-catalyzed cross-coupling of an aryl halide or pseudohalide with an amine

subclasses.name: Copper-catalyzed N-arylation (Ullmann-type)
subclasses.description: Copper-catalyzed coupling of an amine with an aryl halide (Chan-Lam, Ullmann, Goldberg variants)

subclasses.name: Nucleophilic aromatic substitution (SNAr) with N-nucleophiles
subclasses.description: Displacement of a leaving group on an electron-deficient aromatic ring by an amine nucleophile

classes.name: O-arylation
classes.description: Formation of bonds between oxygen atoms and aryl groups

subclasses.name: Pd-catalyzed O-arylation
subclasses.description: Palladium-catalyzed coupling of an alcohol or phenol with an aryl halide to form a diaryl ether or aryl alkyl ether

subclasses.name: Cu-catalyzed O-arylation (Ullmann ether synthesis)
subclasses.description: Copper-catalyzed coupling of a phenol or alcohol with an aryl halide

subclasses.name: SNAr with O-nucleophiles
subclasses.description: Nucleophilic aromatic substitution with an alkoxide or phenoxide on an electron-deficient arene

classes.name: Williamson ether synthesis
classes.description: Formation of ethers via nucleophilic substitution of alkyl halides with alkoxides

superclasses.name: Acylation and Related Processes
superclasses.description: Reactions involving the introduction of acyl groups (R-C=O) into molecules

classes.name: N-acylation
classes.description: Formation of bonds between nitrogen atoms and acyl groups

subclasses.name: Acyl chloride aminolysis
subclasses.description: Reaction of an acyl chloride with an amine to form an amide

subclasses.name: Anhydride aminolysis
subclasses.description: Reaction of a carboxylic acid anhydride with an amine to form an amide

subclasses.name: Carbamate formation (N-acylation with chloroformates)
subclasses.description: Reaction of an amine with a chloroformate to form a carbamate (urethane)

subclasses.name: Urea formation
subclasses.description: Reaction of an amine with an isocyanate or carbonyl diimidazole equivalent to form a urea

classes.name: O-acylation
classes.description: Formation of bonds between oxygen atoms and acyl groups

subclasses.name: Acyl chloride esterification
subclasses.description: Reaction of an alcohol with an acyl chloride to form an ester

subclasses.name: Anhydride esterification
subclasses.description: Reaction of an alcohol with an acid anhydride to form an ester

subclasses.name: Carbonate formation
subclasses.description: Reaction of an alcohol with a chloroformate or related reagent to form a carbonate

classes.name: Friedel-Crafts acylation
classes.description: Electrophilic aromatic substitution with acyl groups

subclasses.name: Friedel-Crafts acylation with acyl halides
subclasses.description: Lewis acid-catalyzed reaction of an arene with an acyl halide to form an aryl ketone

subclasses.name: Friedel-Crafts acylation with anhydrides
subclasses.description: Lewis acid-catalyzed reaction of an arene with a carboxylic acid anhydride to form an aryl ketone

classes.name: Esterification
classes.description: Formation of esters from carboxylic acids and alcohols

subclasses.name: Fischer esterification
subclasses.description: Acid-catalyzed condensation of a carboxylic acid with an alcohol to form an ester

subclasses.name: Steglich esterification
subclasses.description: DCC/DMAP-mediated esterification of a carboxylic acid with an alcohol

subclasses.name: Mitsunobu esterification
subclasses.description: Esterification of a carboxylic acid with an alcohol under Mitsunobu conditions with inversion of configuration

subclasses.name: Transesterification
subclasses.description: Exchange of the alkoxy group of an ester with another alcohol under acid or base catalysis

classes.name: Amide formation
classes.description: Formation of amides from carboxylic acids or derivatives and amines

subclasses.name: Coupling-reagent-mediated amide formation
subclasses.description: Amide bond formation using peptide coupling reagents (e.g., HATU, HBTU, EDC, T3P, CDI)

subclasses.name: Aminolysis of activated esters
subclasses.description: Reaction of an amine with an activated ester (e.g., NHS ester, pentafluorophenyl ester) to form an amide

subclasses.name: Schotten-Baumann reaction
subclasses.description: Amide (or ester) formation by reaction of an acyl chloride with an amine (or alcohol) under aqueous basic biphasic conditions

classes.name: Acid chloride formation
classes.description: Formation of acyl chlorides from carboxylic acids

subclasses.name: Thionyl chloride acyl chloride formation
subclasses.description: Treatment of a carboxylic acid with thionyl chloride (SOCl2) to form an acyl chloride

subclasses.name: Oxalyl chloride acyl chloride formation
subclasses.description: Treatment of a carboxylic acid with oxalyl chloride ((COCl)2) to form an acyl chloride

superclasses.name: Deprotections
superclasses.description: Removal of a protecting group for a molecule

classes.name: Amine deprotection
classes.description: Removal of a protecting group for an amine

subclasses.name: Boc removal
subclasses.description: Removal of a tert-butyloxycarbonyl (Boc) group, typically using acid (e.g., TFA, HCl in dioxane)

subclasses.name: Cbz (Z) removal
subclasses.description: Removal of a benzyloxycarbonyl (Cbz/Z) group, typically by catalytic hydrogenolysis (H2/Pd-C)

subclasses.name: Fmoc removal
subclasses.description: Removal of a 9-fluorenylmethyloxycarbonyl (Fmoc) group, typically using a secondary amine base (e.g., piperidine)

subclasses.name: N-Benzyl removal
subclasses.description: Removal of an N-benzyl group by hydrogenolysis or other methods

subclasses.name: Nosyl/Tosyl removal
subclasses.description: Removal of sulfonamide protecting groups (nosyl, tosyl) from nitrogen, typically using thiolate nucleophiles or dissolving metal conditions

classes.name: Alcohol deprotection
classes.description: Removal of a protecting group for an alcohol

subclasses.name: Silyl ether removal
subclasses.description: Removal of silyl protecting groups (TMS, TBS, TIPS, TBDPS) typically using fluoride sources (TBAF, HF·pyridine) or acidic conditions

subclasses.name: Benzyl ether removal
subclasses.description: Removal of a benzyl (Bn) ether by catalytic hydrogenolysis or Lewis acid conditions

subclasses.name: PMB ether removal
subclasses.description: Removal of a para-methoxybenzyl (PMB) ether using DDQ or acidic conditions

subclasses.name: Acetal/ketal removal
subclasses.description: Acid-catalyzed hydrolysis of acetals or ketals to regenerate the free alcohol and a carbonyl compound

subclasses.name: Acetyl/ester removal from alcohols
subclasses.description: Hydrolysis or methanolysis of an acetate or other ester protecting group on an alcohol

classes.name: Carboxylic acid deprotection
classes.description: Removal of a protecting group for a carboxylic acid

subclasses.name: Methyl/ethyl ester hydrolysis
subclasses.description: Saponification of a methyl or ethyl ester to regenerate the free carboxylic acid

subclasses.name: tert-Butyl ester removal
subclasses.description: Acid-mediated cleavage of a tert-butyl ester (e.g., TFA) to regenerate the carboxylic acid

subclasses.name: Benzyl ester removal
subclasses.description: Removal of a benzyl ester by hydrogenolysis to regenerate the carboxylic acid

classes.name: Carbonyl deprotection
classes.description: Removal of a protecting group for a carbonyl group

subclasses.name: Acetal/thioacetal hydrolysis
subclasses.description: Acid-catalyzed hydrolysis of an acetal or thioacetal to regenerate the aldehyde or ketone

subclasses.name: Dithiane removal
subclasses.description: Removal of a 1,3-dithiane protecting group using mercury(II) salts, NBS, or other electrophilic reagents

classes.name: Hydrogenolysis
classes.description: Removal of a protecting group by hydrogenolysis

subclasses.name: Pd/C-catalyzed hydrogenolysis
subclasses.description: Hydrogenolysis using palladium on carbon catalyst under hydrogen atmosphere

subclasses.name: Transfer hydrogenolysis
subclasses.description: Hydrogenolysis using a hydrogen donor (e.g., ammonium formate, cyclohexadiene) instead of H2 gas

classes.name: Aldol condensation
classes.description: Two carbonyl moieties (of aldehydes or ketones) react to form a β-hydroxyaldehyde or β-hydroxyketone (an aldol reaction), and this is then followed by dehydration to give a conjugated enone

subclasses.name: Aldol reaction (non-dehydrative)
subclasses.description: Reaction of an enolizable carbonyl with an aldehyde to form a β-hydroxy carbonyl product without subsequent dehydration

subclasses.name: Mukaiyama aldol reaction
subclasses.description: Lewis acid-catalyzed aldol reaction between a silyl enol ether and an aldehyde

subclasses.name: Claisen condensation
subclasses.description: Base-catalyzed condensation of two esters (or an ester and a ketone) to form a β-keto ester

subclasses.name: Knoevenagel condensation
subclasses.description: Condensation of an aldehyde or ketone with an active methylene compound in the presence of a base, with dehydration

classes.name: Suzuki coupling
classes.description: Palladium-catalyzed cross-coupling reaction of organoboronic acids or esters with organic halides to form a new carbon-carbon bond

subclasses.name: Suzuki coupling with aryl halides
subclasses.description: Pd-catalyzed coupling of an arylboronic acid/ester with an aryl or heteroaryl halide

subclasses.name: Suzuki coupling with vinyl halides
subclasses.description: Pd-catalyzed coupling of an organoboron species with a vinyl halide to form a substituted alkene

subclasses.name: Suzuki coupling with alkyl substrates
subclasses.description: Pd-catalyzed coupling involving sp3 alkyl boronates or alkyl halides (less common, specialized ligands)

classes.name: Grignard reaction
classes.description: Nucleophilic addition reaction where an organomagnesium halide (Grignard reagent (RMgX)) reacts with a carbonyl to form a new carbon-carbon bond, resulting in the formation of an alcohol

subclasses.name: Grignard addition to aldehydes
subclasses.description: Addition of a Grignard reagent to an aldehyde to form a secondary alcohol

subclasses.name: Grignard addition to ketones
subclasses.description: Addition of a Grignard reagent to a ketone to form a tertiary alcohol

subclasses.name: Grignard addition to esters/CO2
subclasses.description: Addition of a Grignard reagent to an ester (giving tertiary alcohol) or CO2 (giving carboxylic acid)

classes.name: Diels-Alder reaction
classes.description: [4+2] cycloaddition reaction in which a conjugated diene reacts with a dienophile to form a six-membered ring

subclasses.name: Normal electron-demand Diels-Alder
subclasses.description: Cycloaddition of an electron-rich diene with an electron-poor dienophile

subclasses.name: Inverse electron-demand Diels-Alder
subclasses.description: Cycloaddition where the diene is electron-poor and the dienophile is electron-rich

subclasses.name: Hetero-Diels-Alder reaction
subclasses.description: Diels-Alder reaction in which the diene and/or dienophile contains a heteroatom (e.g., N or O)

classes.name: Michael addition
classes.description: Nucleophilic addition reaction where a nucleophile (Michael donor) attacks the β-carbon of an α,β-unsaturated carbonyl compound (Michael acceptor), forming a new carbon-carbon bond

subclasses.name: Enolate Michael addition
subclasses.description: Conjugate addition of a stabilized carbanion or enolate to an α,β-unsaturated carbonyl

subclasses.name: Organocuprate conjugate addition
subclasses.description: 1,4-Addition of an organocuprate (Gilman reagent) to an α,β-unsaturated carbonyl compound

classes.name: Wittig reaction
classes.description: Reaction that converts aldehydes and ketones into alkenes using a phosphonium ylide (Wittig reagent), forming carbon-carbon double bonds

subclasses.name: Classical Wittig reaction
subclasses.description: Olefination using a non-stabilized phosphonium ylide, typically giving (Z)-alkenes

subclasses.name: Horner-Wadsworth-Emmons (HWE) reaction
subclasses.description: Olefination using a stabilized phosphonate carbanion, typically giving (E)-alkenes

subclasses.name: Still-Gennari modification
subclasses.description: Modified HWE reaction using bis(trifluoroethyl) phosphonates to selectively give (Z)-alkenes

classes.name: Heck reaction
classes.description: Reaction of an unsaturated halide (or triflate) with an alkene in the presence of a base and a palladium catalyst to form a substituted alkene

subclasses.name: Intermolecular Heck reaction
subclasses.description: Palladium-catalyzed coupling of an aryl/vinyl halide with an alkene in an intermolecular fashion

subclasses.name: Intramolecular Heck reaction
subclasses.description: Palladium-catalyzed intramolecular cyclization via Heck-type insertion of an alkene

classes.name: Cross coupling reactions
classes.description: Reactions where two different fragments are joined

subclasses.name: Negishi coupling
subclasses.description: Palladium- or nickel-catalyzed cross-coupling of an organozinc reagent with an organic halide

subclasses.name: Stille coupling
subclasses.description: Palladium-catalyzed cross-coupling of an organotin compound (organostannane) with an organic halide

subclasses.name: Kumada coupling
subclasses.description: Nickel- or palladium-catalyzed cross-coupling of a Grignard reagent with an organic halide

subclasses.name: Sonogashira coupling
subclasses.description: Palladium/copper-catalyzed cross-coupling of a terminal alkyne with an aryl or vinyl halide

subclasses.name: Buchwald-Hartwig cross-coupling (C-N)
subclasses.description: Palladium-catalyzed formation of C-N bonds via cross-coupling (also listed under N-arylation)

subclasses.name: Miyaura borylation
subclasses.description: Palladium-catalyzed coupling of an aryl or vinyl halide with a diboron reagent (e.g., B2pin2) to form a boronate ester

subclasses.name: Hiyama coupling
subclasses.description: Palladium-catalyzed cross-coupling of organosilicon compounds with organic halides

subclasses.name: Tsuji-Trost allylation
subclasses.description: Palladium-catalyzed allylic substitution where a nucleophile displaces a leaving group from a π-allylpalladium intermediate

classes.name: Alcohol to alkyl halide
classes.description: Conversion of an alcohol to an alkyl halide

subclasses.name: Appel reaction
subclasses.description: Conversion of an alcohol to an alkyl halide using PPh3 and a tetrahalomethane (e.g., CBr4, CCl4)

subclasses.name: SOCl2/HBr-mediated halide formation
subclasses.description: Treatment of an alcohol with SOCl2, PBr3, or HBr/HCl to form the corresponding alkyl halide

classes.name: Acid to ester
classes.description: Conversion of an acid to an ester

subclasses.name: Acid-catalyzed esterification
subclasses.description: Fischer esterification of a carboxylic acid with an alcohol under acid catalysis

subclasses.name: Diazomethane ester formation
subclasses.description: Methylation of a carboxylic acid with diazomethane (CH2N2) to give a methyl ester

classes.name: Ester to acid
classes.description: Conversion of an ester to an acid

subclasses.name: Base-mediated hydrolysis (saponification)
subclasses.description: Hydrolysis of an ester with aqueous base (e.g., NaOH, LiOH) to give the carboxylate salt

subclasses.name: Acid-mediated ester hydrolysis
subclasses.description: Hydrolysis of an ester under acidic aqueous conditions to give the carboxylic acid

classes.name: Alkene to alkane
classes.description: Conversion of an alkene to an alkane

subclasses.name: Catalytic hydrogenation of alkenes
subclasses.description: Reduction of an alkene to an alkane using H2 and a transition metal catalyst (Pd/C, Pt, Rh, etc.)

subclasses.name: Dissolving metal reduction of alkenes
subclasses.description: Reduction of alkenes using dissolving metals (e.g., Na/NH3, Birch conditions)

classes.name: Ketone to alcohol
classes.description: Conversion of a ketone to an alcohol

subclasses.name: NaBH4 reduction
subclasses.description: Reduction of a ketone to a secondary alcohol using sodium borohydride

subclasses.name: LiAlH4 reduction
subclasses.description: Reduction of a ketone to a secondary alcohol using lithium aluminium hydride

subclasses.name: Asymmetric ketone reduction
subclasses.description: Enantioselective reduction of a ketone using chiral catalysts or reagents (e.g., CBS reduction, Noyori asymmetric hydrogenation)

classes.name: Nitrile to amine
classes.description: Conversion of a nitrile to an amine

subclasses.name: Catalytic hydrogenation of nitriles
subclasses.description: Reduction of a nitrile to a primary amine using H2 with a metal catalyst (Raney Ni, Pd, etc.)

subclasses.name: Hydride reduction of nitriles
subclasses.description: Reduction of a nitrile to a primary amine using LiAlH4 or DIBAL-H

classes.name: Carbonyl reduction
classes.description: Reduction of a carbonyl group

subclasses.name: Aldehyde reduction to alcohol
subclasses.description: Reduction of an aldehyde to a primary alcohol using NaBH4, LiAlH4, or other hydride reagents

subclasses.name: Ketone reduction to alcohol
subclasses.description: Reduction of a ketone to a secondary alcohol using NaBH4, LiAlH4, or other hydride reagents

subclasses.name: Meerwein-Ponndorf-Verley reduction
subclasses.description: Aluminium alkoxide-catalyzed transfer hydrogenation of a ketone or aldehyde to an alcohol

subclasses.name: Asymmetric carbonyl reduction
subclasses.description: Enantioselective reduction of prochiral ketones (CBS, Corey-Bakshi-Shibata; Noyori; biocatalytic, etc.)

subclasses.name: Luche reduction
subclasses.description: Selective 1,2-reduction of an enone using NaBH4 and CeCl3

classes.name: Nitro reduction
classes.description: Reduction of a nitro group

subclasses.name: Catalytic hydrogenation of nitro groups
subclasses.description: Reduction of a nitro group to an amine using H2 and a metal catalyst (Pd/C, Pt, Raney Ni)

subclasses.name: Metal/acid nitro reduction
subclasses.description: Reduction of a nitro group using Fe/HCl, Sn/HCl, or Zn/HCl (Béchamp-type)

subclasses.name: Transfer hydrogenation nitro reduction
subclasses.description: Reduction of a nitro group using a hydrogen-donor (e.g., ammonium formate/Pd-C)

classes.name: Alkene reduction
classes.description: Reduction of an alkene to an alkane

subclasses.name: Heterogeneous catalytic hydrogenation
subclasses.description: Reduction of an alkene using H2 with a heterogeneous catalyst (Pd/C, PtO2, Raney Ni)

subclasses.name: Homogeneous catalytic hydrogenation
subclasses.description: Reduction of an alkene using H2 with a homogeneous catalyst (e.g., Wilkinson's catalyst, Crabtree's catalyst)

subclasses.name: Asymmetric hydrogenation
subclasses.description: Enantioselective reduction of a prochiral alkene using a chiral transition metal catalyst

subclasses.name: Diimide reduction
subclasses.description: Reduction of alkenes using diimide (HN=NH) generated in situ, with syn addition of hydrogen

classes.name: Nitrile reduction
classes.description: Reduction of a nitrile to an amine

subclasses.name: LiAlH4 nitrile reduction
subclasses.description: Reduction of a nitrile to a primary amine using lithium aluminium hydride

subclasses.name: Catalytic hydrogenation of nitriles
subclasses.description: Reduction of a nitrile to a primary amine using H2 and a metal catalyst

classes.name: Ester reduction
classes.description: Reduction of an ester

subclasses.name: LiAlH4 ester reduction
subclasses.description: Full reduction of an ester to a primary alcohol using lithium aluminium hydride

subclasses.name: DIBAL-H partial ester reduction
subclasses.description: Controlled partial reduction of an ester to an aldehyde using DIBAL-H at low temperature

classes.name: Amide reduction
classes.description: Reduction of an amide

subclasses.name: LiAlH4 amide reduction
subclasses.description: Reduction of an amide to an amine using lithium aluminium hydride

subclasses.name: Borane amide reduction
subclasses.description: Reduction of an amide to an amine using borane (BH3·THF or BH3·DMS)

superclasses.name: Functional Group Addition (FGA)
superclasses.description: Addition of a functional group to a molecule

classes.name: Hydroxylation
classes.description: Installation of a hydroxyl group (−OH) into an organic compound

subclasses.name: Dihydroxylation
subclasses.description: Addition of two hydroxyl groups across an alkene (e.g., OsO4-catalyzed Sharpless asymmetric dihydroxylation, KMnO4)

subclasses.name: Hydroboration-oxidation
subclasses.description: Anti-Markovnikov addition of OH to an alkene via hydroboration followed by oxidative workup (H2O2/NaOH)

subclasses.name: Oxymercuration-demercuration
subclasses.description: Markovnikov addition of OH to an alkene via mercuric acetate activation followed by NaBH4 reduction

classes.name: Halogenation
classes.description: Reaction that introduces one or more halogens into a chemical compound

subclasses.name: Chlorination
subclasses.description: Introduction of one or more chlorine atoms into a molecule (e.g., electrophilic, radical, or using NCS, SOCl2)

subclasses.name: Bromination
subclasses.description: Introduction of one or more bromine atoms into a molecule (e.g., electrophilic aromatic bromination with Br2/FeBr3, NBS benzylic/allylic bromination)

subclasses.name: Fluorination
subclasses.description: Introduction of one or more fluorine atoms into a molecule (e.g., DAST, Selectfluor, Deoxo-Fluor, nucleophilic fluorination)

subclasses.name: Iodination
subclasses.description: Introduction of one or more iodine atoms into a molecule (e.g., NIS, I2/oxidant)

subclasses.name: Electrophilic aromatic halogenation
subclasses.description: Halogenation of an aromatic ring by electrophilic aromatic substitution (X2/Lewis acid or NXS reagents)

classes.name: Sulfonation
classes.description: A sulfonic acid group (-SO3H) replaces a hydrogen atom on an aromatic ring

subclasses.name: Aromatic sulfonation
subclasses.description: Electrophilic aromatic substitution with SO3 or fuming H2SO4 to introduce a sulfonic acid group

subclasses.name: Sulfonyl chloride formation
subclasses.description: Formation of sulfonyl chlorides from sulfonic acids or via chlorosulfonation of arenes

classes.name: Nitration
classes.description: Introduction of a nitro group (-NO2) into a molecule, typically by replacing a hydrogen atom

subclasses.name: Electrophilic aromatic nitration
subclasses.description: Nitration of an aromatic ring using mixed acid (HNO3/H2SO4) or acetyl nitrate

subclasses.name: Aliphatic nitration
subclasses.description: Introduction of a nitro group onto an aliphatic carbon (e.g., via radical or Henry reaction-derived intermediates)

classes.name: Carboxylation
classes.description: Reaction where carbon dioxide (CO2) is incorporated into an organic molecule, typically resulting in the formation of a carboxylic acid group (-COOH) or a similar functional group

subclasses.name: Grignard carboxylation
subclasses.description: Reaction of a Grignard reagent with CO2 followed by acidic workup to give a carboxylic acid

subclasses.name: Kolbe-Schmitt reaction
subclasses.description: Carboxylation of a phenoxide with CO2 under pressure to form a hydroxybenzoic acid (salicylic acid synthesis)

subclasses.name: Organolithium carboxylation
subclasses.description: Reaction of an organolithium species with CO2 to form a carboxylic acid after workup

classes.name: Pyridine synthesis
classes.description: Synthesis of pyridine (C5H5N)

subclasses.name: Hantzsch pyridine synthesis
subclasses.description: Multi-component condensation of an aldehyde, two equivalents of a β-ketoester, and ammonia to form a dihydropyridine, followed by oxidation

subclasses.name: Chichibabin pyridine synthesis
subclasses.description: Condensation of three equivalents of an aldehyde with ammonia to form a pyridine

subclasses.name: Kröhnke pyridine synthesis
subclasses.description: Synthesis of pyridines from pyridinium salts, α,β-unsaturated ketones, and ammonium acetate

classes.name: Indole synthesis
classes.description: Synthesis of indole (C8H7N)

subclasses.name: Fischer indole synthesis
subclasses.description: Acid-catalyzed cyclization of an arylhydrazone (from an arylhydrazine and a ketone/aldehyde) to form an indole

subclasses.name: Leimgruber-Batcho indole synthesis
subclasses.description: Two-step synthesis of indoles from o-nitrotoluenes using DMF dimethyl acetal followed by reductive cyclization

subclasses.name: Larock indole synthesis
subclasses.description: Palladium-catalyzed heteroannulation of 2-haloanilines with internal alkynes to form 2,3-disubstituted indoles

subclasses.name: Madelung indole synthesis
subclasses.description: Intramolecular cyclization of an N-acyl-o-toluidine under strongly basic conditions at high temperature

classes.name: Pyrrole synthesis
classes.description: Synthesis of pyrrole (C4H5N)

subclasses.name: Paal-Knorr pyrrole synthesis
subclasses.description: Condensation of a 1,4-dicarbonyl compound with a primary amine (or ammonia) to form a pyrrole

subclasses.name: Knorr pyrrole synthesis
subclasses.description: Condensation of an α-aminoketone with a β-ketoester to form a pyrrole

subclasses.name: Hantzsch pyrrole synthesis
subclasses.description: Reaction of an α-haloketone with a β-ketoester and ammonia to form a pyrrole

classes.name: Furan synthesis
classes.description: Synthesis of furan (C4H4O)

subclasses.name: Paal-Knorr furan synthesis
subclasses.description: Acid-catalyzed cyclodehydration of a 1,4-dicarbonyl compound to form a furan

subclasses.name: Feist-Bénary furan synthesis
subclasses.description: Condensation of a 1,3-dicarbonyl compound with an α-haloketone in the presence of base to form a furan

classes.name: Thiophene synthesis
classes.description: Synthesis of thiophene (C4H4S)

subclasses.name: Paal-Knorr thiophene synthesis
subclasses.description: Reaction of a 1,4-dicarbonyl compound with a sulfurizing agent (e.g., P4S10, Lawesson's reagent) to form a thiophene

subclasses.name: Gewald thiophene synthesis
subclasses.description: Multi-component reaction of a ketone with elemental sulfur and an α-cyanoacetate to form a 2-aminothiophene

classes.name: Oxazole synthesis
classes.description: Synthesis of oxazole (C4H5N)

subclasses.name: Robinson-Gabriel synthesis
subclasses.description: Cyclodehydration of a 2-acylaminocarbonyl compound to form an oxazole

subclasses.name: Van Leusen oxazole synthesis
subclasses.description: Reaction of an aldehyde with tosylmethyl isocyanide (TosMIC) and base to form a 5-substituted oxazole

superclasses.name: Protections
superclasses.description: Addition of a protecting group to a molecule

classes.name: Alcohol protection
classes.description: Addition of a protecting group to an alcohol

subclasses.name: Silyl ether protection
subclasses.description: Protection of an alcohol as a silyl ether (TMS, TBS, TIPS, TBDPS) using the corresponding silyl chloride and a base

subclasses.name: Benzyl ether protection
subclasses.description: Protection of an alcohol as a benzyl ether using BnBr/base or benzyl trichloroacetimidate/acid

subclasses.name: PMB ether protection
subclasses.description: Protection of an alcohol as a para-methoxybenzyl ether

subclasses.name: Acetal/MOM/MEM/THP protection
subclasses.description: Protection of an alcohol as an acetal (methoxymethyl, methoxyethoxymethyl, tetrahydropyranyl ether, etc.)

subclasses.name: Acetyl/ester protection
subclasses.description: Protection of an alcohol as an acetate or other ester (e.g., Ac2O, AcCl, pivaloyl chloride)

classes.name: Amine protection
classes.description: Addition of a protecting group to an amine

subclasses.name: Boc protection
subclasses.description: Protection of an amine as a tert-butyloxycarbonyl carbamate using Boc2O

subclasses.name: Cbz (Z) protection
subclasses.description: Protection of an amine as a benzyloxycarbonyl carbamate using CbzCl

subclasses.name: Fmoc protection
subclasses.description: Protection of an amine as a 9-fluorenylmethyloxycarbonyl carbamate using FmocCl or Fmoc-OSu

subclasses.name: Tosyl/Nosyl protection
subclasses.description: Protection of an amine as a sulfonamide (tosyl or nosyl group)

subclasses.name: Benzyl protection of amines
subclasses.description: Protection of an amine by N-benzylation

classes.name: Carbonyl protection
classes.description: Addition of a protecting group to a carbonyl group

subclasses.name: Acetal/ketal formation
subclasses.description: Protection of an aldehyde or ketone as a cyclic or acyclic acetal/ketal using an alcohol or diol and acid catalyst

subclasses.name: Dithiane/dithiolane formation
subclasses.description: Protection of an aldehyde or ketone as a 1,3-dithiane or 1,3-dithiolane using a dithiol and Lewis acid

classes.name: Carboxylic acid protection
classes.description: Addition of a protecting group to a carboxylic acid

subclasses.name: Methyl/ethyl ester protection
subclasses.description: Protection of a carboxylic acid as a methyl or ethyl ester

subclasses.name: tert-Butyl ester protection
subclasses.description: Protection of a carboxylic acid as a tert-butyl ester (e.g., using isobutylene/H+ or Boc2O/DMAP)

subclasses.name: Benzyl ester protection
subclasses.description: Protection of a carboxylic acid as a benzyl ester (BnOH/coupling or BnBr/base)

classes.name: Thiol protection
classes.description: Addition of a protecting group to a thiol

subclasses.name: S-Trityl protection
subclasses.description: Protection of a thiol as a trityl (triphenylmethyl) thioether

subclasses.name: S-Acm protection
subclasses.description: Protection of a thiol as an acetamidomethyl (Acm) thioether

subclasses.name: S-tBu/S-StBu protection
subclasses.description: Protection of a thiol as a tert-butyl thioether or tert-butyl disulfide

classes.name: Alcohol oxidation
classes.description: Oxidation of alcohols

subclasses.name: Swern oxidation
subclasses.description: Oxidation of an alcohol to an aldehyde or ketone using oxalyl chloride/DMSO and a base (Et3N)

subclasses.name: Dess-Martin periodinane oxidation
subclasses.description: Oxidation of a primary or secondary alcohol to an aldehyde or ketone using Dess-Martin periodinane

subclasses.name: PCC/PDC oxidation
subclasses.description: Oxidation of an alcohol using pyridinium chlorochromate (PCC) or pyridinium dichromate (PDC)

subclasses.name: Jones oxidation
subclasses.description: Oxidation of a primary or secondary alcohol using chromic acid (CrO3/H2SO4), primary alcohols are oxidized to carboxylic acids

subclasses.name: TEMPO-mediated oxidation
subclasses.description: Selective oxidation of primary alcohols to aldehydes (or carboxylic acids) using catalytic TEMPO with a stoichiometric co-oxidant

subclasses.name: IBX oxidation
subclasses.description: Oxidation of alcohols to aldehydes or ketones using 2-iodoxybenzoic acid (IBX)

subclasses.name: Oppenauer oxidation
subclasses.description: Oxidation of a secondary alcohol to a ketone using aluminium alkoxide and an excess of a sacrificial ketone (acetone)

classes.name: Alkene oxidation
classes.description: Oxidation of alkenes

subclasses.name: Dihydroxylation
subclasses.description: Oxidation of an alkene to a 1,2-diol using OsO4 (catalytic with co-oxidant) or KMnO4

subclasses.name: Wacker oxidation
subclasses.description: Palladium-catalyzed oxidation of a terminal alkene to a methyl ketone (Markovnikov selectivity)

subclasses.name: Ozonolysis
subclasses.description: Cleavage of an alkene with ozone to form aldehydes, ketones, or carboxylic acids depending on workup conditions

subclasses.name: Oxidative cleavage (periodate)
subclasses.description: Cleavage of a 1,2-diol with NaIO4 (or Pb(OAc)4) to form two carbonyl fragments

classes.name: Aldehyde oxidation
classes.description: Oxidation of aldehydes

subclasses.name: Pinnick (Lindgren) oxidation
subclasses.description: Oxidation of an aldehyde to a carboxylic acid using NaClO2 with a scavenger for HOCl (e.g., 2-methyl-2-butene)

subclasses.name: KMnO4/CrO3 aldehyde oxidation
subclasses.description: Oxidation of an aldehyde to a carboxylic acid using permanganate or chromium-based oxidants

classes.name: Baeyer-Villiger oxidation
classes.description: Formation of an ester from a ketone or a lactone from a cyclic ketone, using peroxyacids or peroxides as the oxidant

subclasses.name: mCPBA Baeyer-Villiger oxidation
subclasses.description: Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of a ketone using meta-chloroperoxybenzoic acid (mCPBA)

subclasses.name: H2O2-mediated Baeyer-Villiger oxidation
subclasses.description: Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using hydrogen peroxide, often with an acid or enzymatic catalyst

classes.name: Epoxidation
classes.description: Addition of a single oxygen atom across a carbon-carbon double bond, forming an oxirane

subclasses.name: mCPBA epoxidation
subclasses.description: Epoxidation of an alkene using meta-chloroperoxybenzoic acid

subclasses.name: Sharpless asymmetric epoxidation
subclasses.description: Titanium/tartrate-catalyzed enantioselective epoxidation of allylic alcohols using TBHP

subclasses.name: Jacobsen-Katsuki epoxidation
subclasses.description: Manganese-salen-catalyzed asymmetric epoxidation of unfunctionalized cis-alkenes

subclasses.name: Shi epoxidation
subclasses.description: Fructose-derived ketone-catalyzed asymmetric epoxidation of trans- and trisubstituted alkenes using Oxone

subclasses.name: DMDO/Oxone epoxidation
subclasses.description: Epoxidation using dimethyldioxirane (DMDO) generated from acetone and Oxone

classes.name: Sulfide oxidation
classes.description: Oxidation of sulfides

subclasses.name: Sulfide to sulfoxide
subclasses.description: Selective mono-oxidation of a thioether to a sulfoxide (e.g., mCPBA at low temperature, NaIO4, H2O2)

subclasses.name: Sulfide to sulfone
subclasses.description: Full oxidation of a thioether to a sulfone using excess oxidant (e.g., mCPBA, Oxone, H2O2)

superclasses.name: Resolutions
superclasses.description: Separation of a racemic mixture into its respective enantiomers

classes.name: Crystallization
classes.description: Solid formation from a solution, gas, or liquid, resulting in a highly structured crystal with a well-defined three-dimensional lattice

subclasses.name: Preferential crystallization
subclasses.description: Selective crystallization of one enantiomer from a supersaturated solution of a conglomerate

subclasses.name: Classical resolution by diastereomeric crystallization
subclasses.description: Formation of diastereomeric salts or co-crystals with a chiral resolving agent, followed by selective crystallization

classes.name: Enzymatic resolution
classes.description: An enzyme preferentially binds one enantiomer and performs a chemical reaction on it

subclasses.name: Lipase-catalyzed resolution
subclasses.description: Resolution using a lipase that selectively acylates or hydrolyzes one enantiomer of a racemic alcohol or ester

subclasses.name: Protease/amidase-catalyzed resolution
subclasses.description: Resolution using a protease or amidase that selectively hydrolyzes one enantiomer of a racemic amide or amino acid derivative

classes.name: Kinetic resolution
classes.description: Selective transformation of one enantiomer of a racemic mixture into a product at a faster rate than the other enantiomer

subclasses.name: Chemical kinetic resolution
subclasses.description: Use of a chiral chemical catalyst or reagent (non-enzymatic) to selectively react with one enantiomer

subclasses.name: Dynamic kinetic resolution (DKR)
subclasses.description: Combination of in situ racemization of starting material with enantioselective transformation, enabling >50% theoretical yield

classes.name: Chiral chromatography
classes.description: Separation of enantiomers of chiral compounds by chromatographic methods

subclasses.name: Chiral HPLC
subclasses.description: Separation of enantiomers using a chiral stationary phase in high-performance liquid chromatography

subclasses.name: Chiral SFC
subclasses.description: Separation of enantiomers using supercritical fluid chromatography with a chiral stationary phase

subclasses.name: Simulated moving bed (SMB) chromatography
subclasses.description: Continuous chromatographic resolution of enantiomers using a simulated moving bed system for large-scale separation

classes.name: Diastereomeric salt formation
classes.description: Formation of a diastereomeric salt when an enantiomerically pure acid or base reacts with a racemic mixture of the complementary acid or base, creating a mixture of diastereomers

subclasses.name: Chiral acid resolution
subclasses.description: Resolution of a racemic base using an enantiopure chiral acid (e.g., tartaric acid, mandelic acid, camphorsulfonic acid)

subclasses.name: Chiral amine resolution
subclasses.description: Resolution of a racemic acid using an enantiopure chiral amine (e.g., α-methylbenzylamine, quinine, brucine)
